title: Spee

|region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Spee system/Legends|Spee system]]</ref>
*[[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]]<ref name="WesternReaches">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places the [[Spee system/Legends|Spee system]], and therefore Spee itself, in grid square K-20 as part of the [[Saijo sector/Legends|Saijo sector]], an area of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that {{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} places in what ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' designates as the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]].</ref>

{{Quote|Welcome to Thpee, honored thir. Need hotel? Know all good rethtauranth. Act ath guide? Thee many hithtoric thightth?|An alien on Spee, to Roark Garnet|<ref name="TRG1" />}}
'''Spee'''<ref name="EAOC" /><ref name="Fry">{{Twitter|jasoncfry|status/294887358405410816|[[Jason Fry]]|quote=Pretty much." {{C|In response to "the systems in the Atlas's appendix are named after their most prominent planet/celestial body, correct?"}} and "planets not assigned a system in [a] published source can be assumed to reside in [the] system of [the] same name.|archivefile=File:JasonFryAtlas.png|archiveurl=https://archive.is/zThQV}}</ref> was a celestial body<ref name="TRG1" /> located in the [[Spee system/Legends|Spee system]], a part of the [[Saijo sector/Legends|Saijo sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of<ref name="WesternReaches" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was the site of a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] and a number of [[Restaurant/Legends|restaurants]] as well as at least one [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]].<ref name="TRG1" />

At one point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Roark Garnet/Legends|Roark Garnet]] arrived at Spee's spaceport. While he was striding away from the facility, he was followed by an [[Unidentified lizard-like alien|alien]] who welcomed him to Spee and offered their services in finding lodging and providing a guided tour.<ref name="TRG1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Albarrio-VoyagesSF13.png|thumb|right|250px|Spee placed on a map of the Albarrio sector included in ''Voyages SF'' 13.]]
The celestial body referred to by an [[Alien/Legends|alien]]—who pronounced the "s" sound as "th"—as "Thpee" was first mentioned in the [[1987]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|rulebook]] for the first edition of [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="TRG1" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed a location called "Spee" in the [[Albarrio sector/Legends|Albarrio sector]]. Since the article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process, its [[canon]]icity within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity was never confirmed.<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref>

The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently instead placed the Spee [[Star system/Legends|star system]] in grid square K-20 as part of the Saijo [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> [[Jason Fry]],<ref name="Fry" /> co-author of ''The Essential Atlas'',<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> has [[:File:JasonFryAtlas.png|stated]] that the [[Star/Legends|star]] systems listed in the book's Appendix section<ref name="Fry" />—which the Online Companion served as an extension of<ref name="EAOC" />—were named after the most prominent [[Planet/Legends|planets]] or celestial bodies located in those systems. This article therefore considers the celestial body Spee to be named accordingly.<ref name="Fry" />
